cycles.trigger: Review-round pass with the new --check-sources gate; resolved two carry items deferred from v1.3.3.
lessons: CORRECTNESS: references/bituach-leumi-rates.md had the SELF-EMPLOYED National Insurance rates wrong, 5.97% reduced / 17.83% full. Verified against BTL: self-employed NI is 4.47% reduced / 12.83% full. The skill's own evidence claim already had 4.47% right, so the reference file had silently drifted from the evidence. The gate's --check-sources surfaced 17.83% as unjustified.

CARRY RESOLVED (was MAJOR): keren hishtalmut (2.5% employee) is now surfaced in the net flow (new paragraph + Step 5 line), not just a gotcha. It is the biggest real-payslip deduction the default net omits.
CARRY RESOLVED (was MINOR): 2026 minimum wage (6,443.85/month, 35.40/hour from 1.4.2026) added as a Step 0 sanity check.

TWO DEAD SOURCE URLs found and re-pointed: the kolzchut pages for the Section 45a pension credit and for child credit points both now 404. Live content is at חובת_ביטוח_פנסיוני_לעובדים and נקודות_זיכוי_ממס_הכנסה(_להורים_לילדים_בגיל_17-6). The figures themselves (35% on 7% up to 8,148/yr, credit point 242/month) verified correct against the live pages.
VERIFIER FIX (shared): optimization-log.json is now in SKIP_FILES. Its prose ('no 53% error anywhere') was being read as a live 53% claim and blocking the gate. False positive fixed for all skills.

kolzchut is JS-rendered: curl returns only nav/footer chrome (which alone exceeds the 1500-char JS-shell heuristic), so it reads as 'unreachable' rather than 'empty'. Use a browser to read kolzchut article text.
Deferred: the reference file's employee worked-example and the age/pension/disability rate table were CPA-verified in v1.3.0/v1.3.3 and not re-read this cycle.

CORRECTNESS: removed a false arithmetic derivation that framed the 7,703 BL reduced-tier threshold as '60% of the 2026 average wage of 13,769' (60% x 13,769 = 8,261, not 7,703). 7,703 is a separately-published BTL figure. Fixed the reference file, the script comment, and avoided picking between the §1 (13,566) and §2 (13,769) average-wage variants, which are both real BTL figures used for different purposes.

CONSISTENCY: SKILL body/script used 6% severance (mandatory minimum under the pension expansion order) while evidence.json cited 8.33%. Reconciled by stating both in the body + evidence: 6% is the mandatory minimum; 8.33% is the full Section 14 arrangement that discharges statutory severance liability. Calculator keeps 6% (correct default).

CONFIRMED CURRENT 2026 (CPA research): top marginal rate is 50% (47% + 3% surtax) with NO 53% error anywhere (a prior known bug, absent here); Amendment 288 brackets (20% to 19,000, 31% to 25,100, 35% from 25,101); credit point 242/mo; BL employee 4.27% reduced / 12.17% full with the FULL age/pension/disability rate table present; ceiling 51,910; Section 45a pension credit. Net-salary checker: no regeneration needed.

Deferred (MAJOR, over batch cap): surface keren hishtalmut (2.5% employee) as an optional net deduction in the calculator flow, not just a gotcha, it is the biggest real-payslip cash deduction the default net omits. Deferred (MINOR): add the 2026 minimum wage (6,443.85/mo, 35.40/hr from 1.4.2026); add a pension-contribution ceiling; niche BL rows (kibbutz, chalat imputed).

Rate-completeness audit found the bituach-leumi-rates.md reference stated the age-based employee NI rates WRONG ('Under 18: Reduced rates', 'Over retirement: Reduced rates, no health tax') instead of the real 0% (under-18 + pensioner) / 3.93%-10.03% (67-70 deferring) / 3.23%-5.17% (disability). The net-salary tool inherited this and over-charged pensioners/minors. Replaced with the verified employee age/pension table (from the official btl.gov.il employee rate page) in the reference + a flag in SKILL.md Step 3. Legacy skill: backfilled evidence.json (119 facts) from scratch and stripped pre-existing em-dashes.

Fixed top-marginal-rate double-count: references/tax-brackets.md said '50% + 3% mis-summed to 53 percent'; the 50% already includes the 3% mas yesafim surtax (47%+3%). Top rate on salary is 50%. Root cause of a net-salary checker tool bug.

CARRY: bl-rates evidence claim states 67-70 non-pensioner NI '0.61%+3.23%=3.93%' but 0.61+3.23=3.84; the combined 67-70 reduced rate is disputed across sources (3.37/3.84/3.93). Revisit with an authoritative btl render next cycle.

cycles.summary: Corrected child credit points from a flat 1.0/child (+1.5 mother under-5) to the real age-banded table (birth year 2.5, ages 1-2 4.5, age 3 3.5, ages 4-5 2.5, ages 6-17 2 mother/1 father, age 18 0.5 mother). The old values understated parents' credits by 1-3.5 points/child, overstating their tax.
lessons: Child credit points are age-banded AND parent-asymmetric (6-17 is 2 for the mother, 1 for the father); a flat per-child figure is materially wrong, not conservative.
Surfaced by a fresh-eyes audit of the net-salary checker tool, whose credit model was inherited from this reference table.
